name: Accordion

usageGuidance: Import used primitives from @frame-ui/components.
Prefer the preferredSelector value for each primitive.

examples.title: Preview

code.code: npx your-cli add accordion

examples.title: Import the accordion primitives into your component.
examples.description: The text label inherits the trigger typography and foreground styling.

examples.title: Update the import paths to match your project setup.
examples.description: The full trigger row controls spacing, height, and interactive alignment.

code.code: Yes. Triggers can wrap labels, icons, counters, or other inline elements as long as the button stays clear and readable.

examples.title: Token inspector
examples.description: The content panel exposes text color, padding, and motion-related tokens.

Turn borders on when each item should read as a distinct row.

name: Alert
summary: Contextual feedback blocks for status, caution, and system messaging.
usageGuidance: Import used primitives from @frame-ui/components.
Prefer the preferredSelector value for each primitive.

examples.title: Scheduled maintenance tonight
examples.description: Dashboard exports will be briefly unavailable from 23:00 to 23:30 while reporting infrastructure is upgraded.

code.code: npx your-cli add alert
import { FrAlert, FrAlertIcon, FrAlertTitle, FrAlertDescription } from '@frame-ui/components';

examples.title: Heads up
examples.description: Use alerts for important context, status changes, or system guidance that should remain visible near the related workflow.

code.code: import { FrAlert, FrAlertIcon, FrAlertTitle, FrAlertDescription } from '@frame-ui/components';
import { FrAlert, FrAlertIcon, FrAlertTitle, FrAlertDescription } from '@frame-ui/components';

examples.title: Information
examples.description: Share neutral system context, rollout notes, or guidance before someone makes a decision.

code.code: import { FrAlert, FrAlertIcon, FrAlertTitle, FrAlertDescription } from '@frame-ui/components';

Scheduled maintenance tonight

Dashboard exports will be briefly unavailable from 23:00 to 23:30 while reporting infrastructure is upgraded.

examples.title: Success
examples.description: Confirm completed work, successful submissions, or background jobs that finished as expected.

code.code:
Scheduled maintenance tonight

Dashboard exports will be briefly unavailable from 23:00 to 23:30 while reporting infrastructure is upgraded.

Release notes available

Highlight launch details with a softer branded surface while keeping the core alert structure intact.

examples.description: Warn about risky steps, incomplete setup, or edge cases that deserve extra attention.

name: Avatar
summary: Identity visuals with image, fallback, badge, and grouping patterns.
usageGuidance: Import used primitives from @frame-ui/components.
Prefer the preferredSelector value for each primitive.
When both attribute and custom-element selectors exist, prefer the attribute/directive selector.

examples.title: Preview
examples.description: Hover the avatar shell, image, fallback, badge, or group count to inspect the tokens that control sizing, surfaces, overlays, and overlap behavior. Click a region to pin the inspector while you review the current values.

code.code: npx your-cli add avatar

examples.title: Import the avatar primitives your view needs.
examples.description: The root avatar token set controls size, radius, background, border, and default text styling.

examples.title: Token inspector
examples.description: Image presentation is controlled through the shared object-fit token.

code.code: SC

examples.title: Custom Styling Preview
examples.description: Fallback content inherits the root sizing while adding its own internal padding for initials or short labels.

code.code: SC

NS 2
SC JR +1

examples.title: Basic
examples.description: Badges define their own size, color, radius, and border so counts stay legible over the avatar.

code.code:
NS 2
SC JR +1

examples.title: Fallbacks
examples.description: Groups use overlap and motion tokens to control how tightly stacked avatars spread apart on hover.

code.code: SC

examples.title: Sizes
examples.description: The count chip has its own background, border, and foreground tokens for overflow cases.

code.code: SC
JR @

examples.title: Badges
examples.description: Override avatar tokens on a local wrapper when a specific product area needs different radii, badge treatments, or grouping density while keeping the same markup and behaviors.

code.code: JR @
SC MT JR NS

examples.title: Groups
examples.description: This preview applies local token overrides to reshape the avatar, tweak badge sizing, and loosen the group overlap.

code.code: SC MT JR NS
MT 9

import { FrBadge, FrBadgeIcon, FrBadgeLabel, FrBadgeSpinner } from '@frame-ui/components';

name: Button
summary: Action primitives with variants, loading, icon support, and sizing options.
usageGuidance: Import used primitives from @frame-ui/components.
Prefer the preferredSelector value for each primitive.
When both attribute and custom-element selectors exist, prefer the attribute/directive selector.

examples.title: Preview
examples.description: Hover the button shell, icon, label, or loading indicator to inspect the tokens that control spacing, height, radius, icon sizing, and loading treatment. Click a region to pin the inspector while you review the current values.

code.code: npx your-cli add button

examples.title: Import the button primitives your template needs.
examples.description: The root token set defines the button height, padding, typography, radius, and interactive treatment used across appearances.

code.code:
import { tablerArrowRight, tablerPlus, tablerX } from '@ng-icons/tabler-icons';

viewProviders: [provideIcons({ tablerPlus, tablerArrowRight, tablerX })]

examples.title: Register the tabler icons used by your buttons.
examples.description: Icons scale through their own token and adjust automatically with the button size presets.

code.code:
import { tablerArrowRight, tablerPlus, tablerX } from '@ng-icons/tabler-icons';

viewProviders: [provideIcons({ tablerPlus, tablerArrowRight, tablerX })]

examples.title: Token inspector
examples.description: The label inherits the root typography while allowing a separate label weight token when needed.

examples.title: Custom Styling Preview
examples.description: Loading tokens define the spinner size, stroke, and track appearance for both replace and inline loading modes.

examples.title: Basic
examples.description: Override button tokens on a local wrapper when a product area needs a different height, radius, or visual density while keeping the same button API and behavior.

examples.title: Appearances
examples.description: This preview applies local token overrides to adjust height, radius, icon scale, and the filled button surface.

examples.title: Sizes
examples.description: Use the primary button for the main action in a section or flow.

code.code:
<button frButton appearance="primary" type="button">Primary</button>
<button frButton appearance="outline" type="button">Outline</button>
<button frButton appearance="ghost" type="button">Ghost</button>

examples.title: Radius options
examples.description: Switch appearances to control emphasis, from the filled primary action to lighter outline and ghost affordances.

code.code:
<button frButton appearance="primary" type="button">Primary</button>
<button frButton appearance="outline" type="button">Outline</button>
<button frButton appearance="ghost" type="button">Ghost</button>

<button frButton size="sm" type="button">Small</button>
<button frButton size="md" type="button">Medium</button>
<button frButton size="lg" type="button">Large</button>

examples.title: Loading states
examples.description: Use

code.code:
<button frButton size="sm" type="button">Small</button>
<button frButton size="md" type="button">Medium</button>
<button frButton size="lg" type="button">Large</button>

<button frButton radius="none" appearance="outline" type="button">None</button>
<button frButton radius="sm" appearance="outline" type="button">Small</button>
<button frButton radius="md" appearance="outline" type="button">Medium</button>
<button frButton radius="lg" appearance="outline" type="button">Large</button>
<button frButton radius="full" appearance="outline" type="button">Full</button>

examples.title: Icon buttons
examples.description: Use radius presets when a button should feel more squared, softly rounded, or fully pill-shaped.

code.code:
<button frButton radius="none" appearance="outline" type="button">None</button>
<button frButton radius="sm" appearance="outline" type="button">Small</button>
<button frButton radius="md" appearance="outline" type="button">Medium</button>
<button frButton radius="lg" appearance="outline" type="button">Large</button>
<button frButton radius="full" appearance="outline" type="button">Full</button>

examples.title: Anchor hosts
examples.description: Buttons can replace their content with a spinner or keep the label visible and show loading inline, depending on how much continuity the action needs.

name: Button Group
summary: Grouped actions with shared borders and compact horizontal layouts.
usageGuidance: Import used primitives from @frame-ui/components.
Prefer the preferredSelector value for each primitive.
When both attribute and custom-element selectors exist, prefer the attribute/directive selector.

examples.title: Preview
examples.description: Button Group is structural and does not introduce its own component token set. The grouped controls inherit their visual styling from Button, so inspect the grouped button shell, icon, and label to see the button tokens that drive the final result inside the group.

code.code: npx your-cli add button-group

examples.title: Import the button group primitive plus the button building blocks you want to place inside it.
examples.description: Button group itself is structural, so most visual customization happens by scoping button tokens to the grouped children. That keeps the shared borders and collapsed radii while letting you tune the contained buttons.

examples.title: Register the tabler icons used by the grouped actions.
examples.description: This preview applies button-level token overrides inside the group to change height, radius, icon sizing, and border treatment.

examples.title: Token inspector
examples.description: Use a horizontal group when several related actions or view states should read as one compact control cluster.

examples.title: Custom Styling Preview
examples.description: Use vertical orientation when grouped actions stack in sidebars, settings panels, or narrow tool trays.

examples.title: Basic
examples.description: Icon buttons group well for compact display-mode switches or toolbar actions, as long as each button has an accessible label.

examples.title: Vertical
examples.description: You can mix contained button appearances, but use that sparingly so the group still reads as one coordinated control.

name: Calendar
summary: Date and range selection with dropdown captions, week numbers, disabled dates, timezone formatting, and RTL support.
usageGuidance: Import used primitives from @frame-ui/components.
Prefer the preferredSelector value for each primitive.

examples.title: Preview
examples.description: Inspect the calendar shell, navigation, caption, weekday labels, day cells, selected state, and range state.

code.code: npx your-cli add calendar
import { FrCalendar } from '@frame-ui/components';

examples.title: Import the calendar component.
examples.description: The calendar root controls surface, border, radius, padding, typography, and cell sizing.

code.code: import { FrCalendar } from '@frame-ui/components';
date = new FormControl<Date | null>(new Date());

examples.title: Token inspector
examples.description: Navigation buttons use compact square controls with hover and focus treatment.

code.code: date = new FormControl<Date | null>(new Date());
<frame-calendar [formControl]="date" />

examples.title: Custom Styling Preview
examples.description: The caption inherits calendar foreground color and establishes the visible month.

code.code: <frame-calendar [formControl]="date" />

examples.title: Basic
examples.description: Weekday labels use the calendar cell size and muted foreground color.

code.code: date: Date | null = new Date(2026, 5, 10);

examples.title: Range Calendar
examples.description: Day cells define the interactive hit target, radius, hover color, and focus ring.

code.code: date: Date | null = new Date(2026, 5, 10);
<frame-calendar [selected]="date" (selectedChange)="date = $any($event)" />

examples.title: Month and Year Selector
examples.description: Selected range endpoints use the selected day background and foreground colors.

code.code: <frame-calendar [selected]="date" (selectedChange)="date = $any($event)" />

examples.title: Presets
examples.description: Middle range cells use a subtle background band behind the day button.

code.code:
<frame-calendar
  locale="de-DE"
  mode="range"
  [firstDayOfWeek]="1"
  [month]="month"
  [selected]="range"
  (selectedChange)="range = $any($event)"
/>

examples.title: Date and Time Picker
examples.description: Disabled dates are unavailable for selection and use reduced opacity plus decoration.

code.code:
<frame-calendar
  locale="de-DE"
  mode="range"
  [firstDayOfWeek]="1"
  [month]="month"
  [selected]="range"
  (selectedChange)="range = $any($event)"
/>
month = new Date(2026, 5, 1);

examples.title: Disabled dates
examples.description: Date labels render compact metadata below the day number.

code.code: month = new Date(2026, 5, 1);

<frame-calendar
  captionLayout="dropdown"
  [month]="month"
  [fromYear]="1926"
  [toYear]="2026"
/>

examples.title: Custom Cell Size
examples.description: Override calendar tokens locally to tune the surface, selected day color, focus ring, navigation controls, or cell size.

code.code:
<frame-calendar
  captionLayout="dropdown"
  [month]="month"
  [fromYear]="1926"
  [toYear]="2026"
/>

date: Date | null = new Date(2026, 5, 10);
tomorrow = new Date(2026, 5, 7);

examples.title: Open from select
examples.description: This preview increases the cell size and uses the built-in date label slot.

code.code:
date: Date | null = new Date(2026, 5, 10);
tomorrow = new Date(2026, 5, 7);

<frame-calendar [selected]="date" (selectedChange)="date = $any($event)" />

<button frButton appearance="outline" type="button" (click)="date = tomorrow">
  Tomorrow
</button>

examples.title: Week Numbers
examples.description: A single-date calendar with native button cells and month navigation.

code.code:
<frame-calendar [selected]="date" (selectedChange)="date = $any($event)" />

<button frButton appearance="outline" type="button" (click)="date = tomorrow">
  Tomorrow
</button>

examples.title: Selected Date With Time Zone
examples.description: Set mode=

code.code:
<frame-calendar [selected]="date" (selectedChange)="date = $any($event)" />
<input type="time" [(ngModel)]="startTime" />
<input type="time" [(ngModel)]="endTime" />

examples.title: RTL support
examples.description: Use captionLayout=

code.code:
<frame-calendar [selected]="date" (selectedChange)="date = $any($event)" />
<input type="time" [(ngModel)]="startTime" />
<input type="time" [(ngModel)]="endTime" />

name: Checkbox
summary: Native form-compatible boolean input with token-based styling.
usageGuidance: Import used primitives from @frame-ui/components.
Prefer the preferredSelector value for each primitive.

examples.title: Preview
examples.description: Hover the field wrapper, checkbox box, or label to inspect the tokens that control sizing, checked states, label spacing, and invalid treatment. Click a region to pin the inspector while you review the current values.

code.code: npx your-cli add checkbox
import { FrCheckbox, FrCheckboxField, FrCheckboxLabel } from '@frame-ui/components';

examples.title: Import the checkbox primitives for the input, field wrapper, and label.
examples.description: The field wrapper is responsible for layout spacing between the control and its label, and it also inherits the label color contract.

code.code: import { FrCheckbox, FrCheckboxField, FrCheckboxLabel } from '@frame-ui/components';
import { FrCheckbox, FrCheckboxField, FrCheckboxLabel } from '@frame-ui/components';

examples.title: Token inspector
examples.description: The checkbox box tokens control its size, border, radius, checked fill, focus ring, and invalid border treatment.

code.code: import { FrCheckbox, FrCheckboxField, FrCheckboxLabel } from '@frame-ui/components';

examples.title: Custom Styling Preview
examples.description: Indicator sizing for both the checkmark and indeterminate bar comes from the indicator and indeterminate dimension tokens.

examples.title: Basic
examples.description: Labels follow the checkbox field spacing and color tokens while keeping typography intentionally simple and native.

examples.title: Checked and unchecked
examples.description: Customize the checkbox by scoping token overrides to a wrapper or form section. This keeps the checkbox semantics intact while letting you adjust size, spacing, and checked treatment locally.

examples.title: Indeterminate
